title: Aventures de Monsieur Pickwick, Vol. II
summaries: "Aventures de Monsieur Pickwick, Vol. II" by Charles Dickens is a novel serialized from March 1836 to November 1837. The story follows Samuel Pickwick and his fellow club members on comic adventures through the English countryside. Their journeys lead to romantic misunderstandings, eccentric encounters with rogues and servants, and culminate in one of literature's most famous legal cases when Pickwick's landlady sues him for breach of promise. This publishing phenomenon popularized serialized fiction and became a cornerstone of modern entertainment. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: England -- Social life and customs -- 19th century -- Fiction
